question.text: What is the role of FATF-Style Regional Bodies (FSRBs) in the global AML/CFT framework?

answer.text: FSRBs promote the adoption and implementation of FATF Recommendations within their respective regions and conduct mutual evaluations of member countries
FSRBs have the authority to directly prosecute money laundering cases in their member countries
FSRBs operate independently from FATF and set their own separate AML/CFT standards
FSRBs are limited to providing financial assistance to developing countries for AML programs

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Nine FSRBs have been established globally (including APG and EAG, of which India is a member). FSRBs promote the adoption and implementation of FATF Recommendations within their regions, conduct mutual evaluations of their members, and contribute to the global AML/CFT effort through regional cooperation. **The Trap:** Option B wrongly suggests FSRBs set separate standards — they work within the FATF framework and promote the same 40 Recommendations, adapted for regional contexts. **Cross-Exam Context:** India's membership in APG and EAG as FSRBs is tested in NISM IFSCA-01. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** India is a member of two FSRBs (APG and EAG) in addition to being a FATF member — mutual evaluations by these bodies directly impact India's regulatory framework and consequently your compliance obligations.
